# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for Cloud Storage bucket that stores application backups. Use default settings without versioning or lifecycle management to keep configuration simple. This is for our backup storage that we'll enhance later based on retention requirements.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

Resource config:
{
    "name": "gcp-cloud-storage-bucket",
    "properties": {
        "bucketName": "${var.project}-buckets",
        "location": "${var.defaultLocation}",
        "versioningEnabled": false,
        "lifecycleManagement": [
            {
                "rule": {
                    "action": {
                        "type": "Delete"
                    },
                    "condition": {
                        "age": 365
                    }
                }
            }
        ],
        "storageClass": "STANDARD",
        "cors": [],
        "requesterPays": false
    }
}

Resource config without variable reference:
{
    "name": "gcp-cloud-storage-bucket",
    "properties": {
        "bucketName": "app-test-buckets",
        "location": "${var.defaultLocation}",
        "versioningEnabled": true,
        "lifecycleManagement": [
            {
                "rule": {
                    "action": {
                        "type": "Delete"
                    },
                    "condition": {
                        "age": 365
                    }
                }
            }
        ],
        "storageClass": "STANDARD",
        "cors": [],
        "requesterPays": false
    }
}

## End of Requirement ##

Deployment Manager allows users to declare resources and other management tasks in YAML, JSON or XML syntax. You can refer to existing templates and modify them for your needs. However, you cannot make any modifications directly in the template file itself; instead, you must use the Deployment Manager Console.
